homepage.title: No Trust/Eco Badge Bar on Homepage
homepage.header: Adding a trust badge bar with eco certifications above the fold can increase purchase confidence by 12-15% -- Catalyst Pet's sustainability story is buried below the fold
homepage.benchmark_label: Tuft + Paw -- Mobile PDP Trust Badges
observations: Catalyst Pet's homepage hero section ('Keep the Cat, Ditch the Smell') has no trust badges, eco certifications, or key differentiator icons above the fold
Key selling points like 'Biodegradable', 'Upcycled Soft Wood', 'Made in America', 'Low Dust' are not surfaced until users scroll far down the page
For an eco-conscious brand competing against traditional clay litter, sustainability credentials need to be instantly visible to justify the premium price
Tuft + Paw surfaces 'Low-Tracking', 'Flushable', '99% Dust-Free' as icon badges directly below the product title, making benefits scannable in seconds
recommendations: Add a horizontal trust badge bar directly below the hero section with 4-5 key icons: Biodegradable, Upcycled Wood, Low Dust, Made in USA, Lightweight
Use simple icon + 2-3 word format (similar to Tuft + Paw) so mobile users can scan benefits without reading paragraphs
Consider adding third-party eco certification badges (BPI Certified, Sustainable Forestry) to build credibility beyond self-claimed benefits
homepage.benchmark_tag: Standard -- 9/10 stores have trust badge bars

homepage.title: Hero Section Lacks Quantified Value Proposition
homepage.header: Quantifying key benefits in the hero section can boost engagement rate by 8-12% -- visitors need instant reasons to stay
homepage.benchmark_label: Tuft + Paw -- Mobile Hero with Clear Benefits
observations: The headline 'Keep the Cat, Ditch the Smell' is catchy but does not communicate any specific product benefit or differentiator
There are no quantified stats, review counts, or social proof elements visible in the first fold
The formula selector below the hero (Healthy Cat, Multi Cat, Unscented, Wood Pellets) provides good navigation but lacks price anchoring
Tuft + Paw immediately communicates 'Low-tracking, low-dust, multi-cat strength' in the hero subtitle, setting clear expectations
recommendations: Add 2-3 quantified stats below the headline: '5,200+ 5-Star Reviews', '7x Lighter Than Clay', '57% Savings on Subscription'
Include a mini social proof bar with aggregate Yotpo rating (4.6 stars, 5,239 reviews) near the CTA
Add subscription price anchoring on the hero: 'Starting at $12.35/month' to pre-qualify price-sensitive visitors
homepage.benchmark_tag: Standard -- 10/10 stores have quantified hero messaging

homepage.title: No Sticky Announcement Bar or Free Shipping Threshold
homepage.header: A persistent free shipping bar can increase average order value by 5-10% by motivating threshold-based purchases
observations: Catalyst Pet's announcement bar shows Walmart availability, which is useful but not conversion-oriented for the D2C store
There is no free shipping threshold messaging despite offering free shipping on all orders -- this benefit is hidden until the PDP/cart
The announcement bar text is quite long (2 lines on mobile) and contains two separate 'here' links, creating cognitive load
PetSmart uses a bold, concise promotional bar ('15% OFF $50 Pickup or Same-Day Delivery') that drives immediate action
recommendations: Replace the Walmart bar with a rotating announcement: 'Free Shipping on Every Order' / 'Subscribe & Save Up to 57%' / 'Now at Walmart -- Find a Store'
Make the bar sticky on scroll so the value proposition persists as users browse
Keep each message to one line on mobile with a single clear CTA
homepage.benchmark_tag: Growing -- 6/10 stores have free shipping threshold bars

homepage.title: Homepage Missing Bestseller/Product Cards Section
homepage.header: Showing products with prices and subscription savings on the homepage can boost PDP view rate by 15-20% -- visitors currently see no products or prices
observations: The homepage has no product cards with images, prices, or quick-add buttons -- visitors must click through to PDP or collection to see any pricing
The mid-section is dedicated to a comparison chart (Catalyst vs Clay) which is educational but delays the purchase path
Homepage formula selector buttons link to individual PDPs but show no price preview, making the shopping experience feel disconnected
Tuft + Paw shows products with clear pricing ($29.00) and descriptions directly in the collection, reducing clicks to purchase
recommendations: Add a 'Shop Our Formulas' section with product cards showing: image, formula name, star rating, review count, subscription price (e.g., 'From $12.35/mo'), and 'Shop Now' CTA
Show the subscription savings visually (e.g., strikethrough on one-time price) to anchor value at the discovery stage
Position this section above the comparison chart so price-ready visitors can act immediately
homepage.benchmark_tag: Standard -- 8/10 stores show products with prices on homepage

homepage.title: Klaviyo Popup Covers Content on Mobile
homepage.header: Intrusive popups on mobile can increase bounce rate by 10-15% -- the email capture overlay blocks the entire viewport
homepage.client_label: Catalyst Pet -- Klaviyo Popup Blocking Content
observations: The Klaviyo email capture popup ('Enter for a chance to win one free bag of litter!') appears while scrolling and covers the entire mobile viewport
The popup has no visible close button from the screenshot, forcing users to interact with it before continuing to browse
It appears mid-scroll rather than on exit intent, interrupting users who are actively engaged with content (reviews section)
The popup triggers repeatedly during a single session, appearing even after being dismissed
recommendations: Delay the popup to exit-intent only on mobile, or trigger after 60+ seconds of engaged browsing
Ensure the close button (X) is large and clearly visible on mobile (minimum 44x44px tap target)
Consider replacing the full-screen popup with a non-intrusive slide-up banner at the bottom of the screen
Limit popup frequency to once per session with a cookie-based cooldown
homepage.benchmark_tag: Standard -- intrusive popups are a known anti-pattern

collection.title: No Prices Shown on Collection Product Cards
collection.header: Displaying prices on collection cards reduces friction and can increase add-to-cart rate by 8-12% -- visitors currently must click through to see any pricing
collection.benchmark_label: PetSmart -- Collection Cards with Prices & Ratings
observations: Catalyst Pet's collection cards show formula name, star rating (4.6), review count, and description -- but no price whatsoever
Visitors cannot compare value across formulas without clicking into each PDP individually, adding unnecessary friction to the purchase path
The subscription savings (up to 57%) are completely invisible at the collection level, missing a major conversion opportunity
PetSmart shows price ranges ($13.99 - $19.99), sizes, ratings, and review counts on every product card for instant comparison
recommendations: Add price to every collection card -- show both one-time and subscription price (e.g., '$23.49/mo or $28.50 one-time')
Display subscription savings badge (e.g., 'Save 57%') on cards to encourage subscription at the discovery stage
Consider adding a quick 'Subscribe' CTA button on each card to reduce the path to checkout
collection.benchmark_tag: Standard -- 10/10 stores show prices on collection cards

collection.title: No Filter or Sort Options on Collection Page
collection.header: Collection filtering and sorting options help visitors find the right product faster -- their absence adds friction for multi-cat or budget-conscious shoppers
collection.client_label: Catalyst Pet -- No Filters on Collection
observations: The collection page has no sort or filter options -- products are displayed in a fixed order with no way to sort by price, rating, or popularity
While the catalog is small (4 litter formulas), there is no way to filter by use case (single cat vs multi-cat) or scent preference
PetSmart provides 'Sort & filter', 'Shop by store', 'Brand', and 'Price' filter options that let shoppers narrow down from 141+ results efficiently
As Catalyst expands their product line (waste bags, starter packs, accessories), the lack of filtering will become a bigger pain point
recommendations: Add a basic sort dropdown (Popularity, Price Low-High, Price High-Low, Newest) even for the current small catalog
Add filter tags by use case: 'Best for 1 Cat', 'Best for Multi-Cat', 'Unscented', 'Non-Clumping'
Implement a 'Help Me Choose' button that links to the existing quiz for undecided visitors

collection.title: No Quick-Add or Subscribe Button on Collection Cards
collection.header: Adding quick-action buttons on collection cards can reduce clicks-to-purchase by 40% and boost conversion for returning customers
collection.client_label: Catalyst Pet -- Collection Card (Shop Now Only)
collection.benchmark_label: Tuft + Paw -- Collection Card with Price & Description
observations: Each product card has only a 'SHOP NOW' link that navigates to the full PDP, requiring multiple steps before a returning customer can reorder
There is no quick-add-to-cart functionality on the collection page, even for repeat subscribers who already know their preferred formula
The card layout is image-heavy with large product photos but small text descriptions, making it difficult to compare formulas at a glance
Tuft + Paw includes clear pricing and concise product descriptions on collection cards for faster decision-making
recommendations: Add a 'Quick Subscribe' button on each card that auto-selects the most popular size/frequency for that formula
For returning logged-in customers, show a 'Reorder' button with their previous subscription configuration
Consider adding a compact comparison view or size selector directly on the card
collection.benchmark_tag: Growing -- 6/10 stores have quick-add on collection cards

pdp.title: No Sticky Add-to-Cart on Mobile PDP
pdp.header: A sticky Add to Cart bar on mobile can boost conversions by 3-5% -- once users scroll past the ATC button, there is no way to purchase without scrolling back up
pdp.client_label: Catalyst Pet -- No Sticky ATC on Scroll
pdp.benchmark_label: Tuft + Paw -- Sticky ATC with Subscription Option
observations: When scrolling past the ATC button on the PDP, there is no sticky bar that follows the user -- the ATC button disappears from view
The PDP is extremely long (price comparison table, benefits summary, recommended by section, video, reviews, 18 FAQs) requiring extensive scrolling
Users who scroll down to read reviews or FAQs and become convinced must scroll all the way back up to the ATC section to complete their purchase
Tuft + Paw keeps the ATC button and key purchase info (price, subscription toggle) persistently visible as users explore the page
recommendations: Add a sticky bottom bar on mobile that appears after the user scrolls past the main ATC button
Include product name, selected price, and a prominent 'Add to Cart' CTA in the sticky bar
Show the selected purchase type (Subscribe or One-Time) in the sticky bar to reduce confusion
pdp.benchmark_tag: Growing -- 5/10 stores have sticky ATC on mobile

pdp.title: Subscription Not Pre-Selected on PDP
pdp.header: Pre-selecting the subscription option on PDP can increase subscription adoption by 15-25% -- most US D2C pet brands default to Subscribe & Save
pdp.client_label: Catalyst Pet -- Subscribe & Save Selection Area
pdp.benchmark_label: Tuft + Paw -- Subscribe & Save Pre-Selected (25% Off)
observations: The PDP shows 'Subscribe & Save' and 'One-Time Purchase' as options in Step 2, but it is unclear which is pre-selected by default on fresh page load
The subscription savings are shown as '57% over a one-time purchase' only after selecting the 40lb option -- the initial savings are not immediately visible for smaller sizes
Tuft + Paw pre-selects 'Subscribe & Save 25%' by default with a clear strikethrough price ($29 to $21.75) and prominent savings callout
The multi-step flow (4 steps: formula, purchase type, cat count, delivery) adds friction compared to competitors who use a simple toggle
recommendations: Pre-select 'Subscribe & Save' by default for all formulas, with the most popular delivery size highlighted as 'Most Popular' or 'Best Value'
Show percentage savings prominently next to the subscription toggle (e.g., 'Subscribe & Save 25-57%') before users make a selection
Consider simplifying the 4-step flow to 2 steps: size selector + Subscribe/One-Time toggle -- the cat count can be a helper tooltip rather than a required step

pdp.title: No Sustainability/Eco Certification Badges Near ATC
pdp.header: Displaying eco-certification badges near the Add to Cart button can increase purchase confidence by 10-15% for sustainability-conscious buyers
pdp.client_label: Catalyst Pet -- ATC Area (No Eco Badges)
pdp.benchmark_label: Open Farm -- Certified B Corp Badge
observations: The ATC area shows 'Free Shipping with any order!' and a product illustration but has no eco/sustainability badges or certifications
Key environmental claims (biodegradable, upcycled soft wood, renewable) are mentioned only in paragraph text far below the fold
The 'Made in America' and 'Donations to Shelters' badges appear only in the cart drawer, not on the PDP where the purchase decision is made
Open Farm prominently displays their Certified B Corporation badge, reinforcing their sustainability commitment at every touchpoint
recommendations: Add 3-4 eco certification icons directly below or beside the ATC button: Biodegradable, Upcycled Wood, Made in USA, Low Dust
If Catalyst Pet has any third-party certifications (BPI Certified Compostable, Sustainable Forestry Initiative), feature them prominently
Include a 'Why Sustainable?' expandable section near the ATC with quantified environmental impact stats
pdp.benchmark_tag: Standard -- 8/10 stores show certification badges on PDP

pdp.title: No Money-Back Guarantee or Risk Reversal Near ATC
pdp.header: Displaying a money-back guarantee near the ATC can reduce purchase hesitation by 18-22% -- first-time litter switchers need reassurance
pdp.benchmark_label: Tuft + Paw -- 30 Day Money-Back Guarantee Badge
observations: There is no satisfaction guarantee, money-back promise, or risk-reversal messaging visible on the PDP near the purchase decision point
Switching cat litter is a high-anxiety purchase -- cats may reject the new litter, and owners need reassurance that they can return it
Tuft + Paw prominently displays '30 Day Money-Back Guarantee' as an icon badge directly below their ATC button alongside 'Free Shipping' and 'Cancel Anytime'
Catalyst has a refund policy page (linked in footer) but the guarantee is not surfaced where the purchase decision is being made
recommendations: Add a '100% Satisfaction Guarantee' or '30-Day Money-Back' badge directly below the ATC button
Display 3 trust icons in a row: Free Shipping + Cancel Anytime + Money-Back Guarantee
Include a brief one-line reassurance: 'If your cat doesn't love it, we'll make it right' to reduce litter-switching anxiety
pdp.benchmark_tag: Growing -- 5/10 stores show money-back guarantee on PDP

pdp.title: Product Description Lacks Collapsible Sections
pdp.header: Using accordion/collapsible sections for PDP content improves scannability by 30-40% -- the current layout mixes educational and purchase content in a single long scroll
pdp.client_label: Catalyst Pet -- Benefits & Recommended By Sections
pdp.benchmark_label: Tuft + Paw -- Clean PDP with Scannable Layout
observations: The PDP content is presented as a single continuous scroll: benefits list, 'Recommended By' logo carousel (50+ rescue organizations), comparison image, savings table, mission video, reviews, and 18 FAQs
There are no collapsible/accordion sections to help users find specific information -- they must scroll through everything sequentially
The 'Recommended By' section alone contains 50+ organization logos that take up significant vertical space on mobile
The FAQ section has 18 items all visible at once, creating an overwhelming wall of text
recommendations: Organize PDP content into collapsible accordion sections: Benefits, Ingredients/Materials, How It Works, Recommended By, Savings Calculator, FAQ
Show the first 2-3 FAQ items expanded by default and collapse the rest behind a 'Show More' toggle
Limit the 'Recommended By' section to 5-6 key logos with a 'See All Partners' expandable section
pdp.benchmark_tag: Standard -- 9/10 stores use accordion sections on PDP

pdp.title: No Yotpo Review Photo/Video Gallery on PDP
pdp.header: User-generated review photos and videos can increase conversion by 10-15% -- Catalyst has 2,151+ reviews but no visual UGC gallery on the PDP
pdp.client_label: Catalyst Pet -- PDP Below Fold (No UGC)
pdp.benchmark_label: Tuft + Paw -- 10,260 Reviews with Photo Gallery
observations: Catalyst Pet has 2,151 reviews for Healthy Cat formula and 5,239 across all products -- a significant social proof asset
However, the Yotpo widget is configured for text-only reviews at the bottom of the page with no photo/video gallery section
There is no UGC (User Generated Content) section showing real cats using the product, which is especially compelling for pet products
The reviews are positioned at the very bottom of a very long PDP, requiring extensive scrolling to reach them
recommendations: Enable Yotpo's photo/video review gallery and position a UGC carousel in the mid-PDP section, before the FAQ
Encourage photo reviews through a post-purchase email flow offering a discount for photo submissions
Add a 'Reviews' anchor link near the star rating at the top of the PDP for quick navigation to the review section
pdp.benchmark_tag: Growing -- 7/10 stores feature UGC review galleries

cart.title: Cart Drawer Missing Express Checkout Buttons
cart.header: Displaying express checkout options (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Shop Pay) in the cart drawer can reduce checkout time by 40% and boost conversion by 5-8%
cart.benchmark_label: Tuft + Paw -- ATC with Express Payment Badges
observations: The cart drawer mentions 'Express Payments are available at Checkout' as text but does not display the actual payment method icons (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Shop Pay, PayPal)
Shoppers who prefer express checkout have no visual cue that their preferred method is available, potentially causing them to abandon before reaching checkout
Showing payment icons builds trust and signals a modern, frictionless checkout experience
The Afterpay/BNPL option detected in the page source is not mentioned anywhere in the cart drawer either
recommendations: Replace the text 'Express Payments are available at Checkout' with actual payment method icons: Apple Pay, Google Pay, Shop Pay, PayPal
Add Afterpay/Klarna BNPL messaging (e.g., '4 interest-free payments of $12.35') to reduce price sensitivity
Consider adding a 'Buy Now' express checkout button directly in the cart drawer for one-click purchase
cart.benchmark_tag: Standard -- 8/10 stores show express payment icons in cart

cart.title: No Cart Upsell for Subscription Upgrade
cart.header: Offering a subscription upgrade prompt in the cart can increase subscription adoption by 10-15% -- one-time buyers should see the savings opportunity
observations: The cart drawer shows 'You May Also Like' cross-sell products (Biodegradable Pet Waste Bags, Starter Pack) which is good
However, there is no prompt to upgrade a one-time purchase to a subscription with savings callout
The cart shows a 30% off coupon (CAT30) for waste bags but no incentive to subscribe for the main litter product
For a subscription-first brand, the cart is a critical conversion point to move one-time buyers into the recurring revenue model
recommendations: Add a 'Switch to Subscribe & Save' banner in the cart showing the per-order savings and subscription benefits
Display a comparison: 'One-Time: $49.40 vs Subscribe: $28.50/delivery (Save 42%)' with a one-tap conversion toggle
For existing subscribers, show a 'Add to Next Delivery' option instead of creating a separate one-time order
cart.benchmark_tag: Growing -- 5/10 subscription brands offer cart upgrade prompts

cart.title: No Estimated Delivery Date in Cart
cart.header: Showing estimated delivery dates in the cart can reduce checkout abandonment by 7-10% -- shoppers want to know when their litter will arrive
observations: The cart drawer shows subtotal and free shipping qualification but no estimated delivery date or shipping speed information
For a consumable product like cat litter, knowing delivery timing is critical -- customers may be running low and need to plan
The PDP FAQ mentions shipping details but this information should surface in the cart where the purchase decision is finalized
Adding delivery estimates increases confidence and reduces 'will it arrive in time?' anxiety
recommendations: Add an estimated delivery date range (e.g., 'Arrives Mar 25-27') based on the customer's zip code or default to 3-5 business day estimate
For subscription orders, show the next delivery date to set expectations for the recurring schedule
Consider adding expedited shipping options with clear pricing for customers who need litter urgently
cart.benchmark_tag: Growing -- 6/10 stores show delivery estimates in cart
